description: This chart illustrates the relationship between logK<sub>MnS</sub>, logQ<sub>MnS</sub>, and solidification fraction z<sub>s</sub> for three Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steel samples: 1# with 0.87 wt% Al, 2# with 2.77 wt% Al, 3# with 4.70 wt% Al. For each sample, the solid line stands for logK<sub>MnS</sub> and the dashed line stands for logQ<sub>MnS</sub>, their intersection point indicates the critical solidification fraction at which MnS inclusions begin to precipitate. The intersection points locate at z<sub>s</sub>=0.77 for 1# sample, 0.81 for 2# sample, and 0.85 for 3# sample. The core conclusion supported by this figure is that higher Al content increases the critical solidification fraction for MnS precipitation, meaning increasing Al content delays the precipitation of MnS inclusions during steel solidification, which is consistent with the solidification route calculation results of the tested steels.
